source: Learning bash with Rocky - Conditional Structures (if and case): The case ... in / esac multi-branch conditional

questions.q: What does a case structure evaluate?
options: A single variable, compared against listed values
questions.why: case $variable in ... esac compares the variable to each value.

questions.q: Which keyword closes a case structure?
What terminates the commands of each case branch?
questions.why: The commands between ) and ;; run for the first matching value.

questions.q: What does the * branch do in a case?
options: Runs for all values not matched by an earlier branch (the default)
questions.why: * at the end is the catch-all default branch.

questions.q: How do you match "yes" OR "YES" in one case branch?
questions.why: | separates alternative values; wildcards like [Yy][Ee][Ss] also work.
